name: Sol
description: Sol, also known as Sól or Sunna, is the Norse goddess of the Sun. She is the daughter of Mundilfari and sister of Máni, the god of the Moon. Her primary duty is to drive the chariot of the sun across the sky each day, bringing light and warmth to the world. The chariot is pulled by the horses Árvakr (Early Awake) and Alsviðr (Very Quick). To protect Sol and the world from the intense heat, the gods placed a shield called Svalinn (Cooler) in front of her. Sol is constantly pursued by the wolf Sköll, who seeks to devour her. According to prophecy, Sköll will eventually succeed in swallowing the sun, marking the beginning of Ragnarök, the twilight of the gods. Despite the grim fate that awaits her, Sol bravely fulfills her duty each day, ensuring the cycle of day and night continues. She is revered for her life-giving warmth and the vital role she plays in the natural world. After Ragnarök, a new sun, perhaps her daughter, will rise to continue the cycle. Some accounts suggest she has daughters who will carry on her legacy. Sol represents light, warmth, life, and the cyclical nature of time. Her existence is a constant reminder of the impending doom of Ragnarök, yet also a symbol of hope for renewal and rebirth.
subtitle: The Norse Goddess of the Sun

keyMyths.title: The Creation of Sun and Moon
keyMyths.description: Mundilfari named his children Sun and Moon, angering the gods. As punishment, they were tasked with driving the sun and moon across the sky.

keyMyths.title: The Pursuit of Sköll
keyMyths.description: Sol is constantly chased by the wolf Sköll, who seeks to devour her. This chase explains the cyclical nature of day and night.

keyMyths.title: Sol at Ragnarök
keyMyths.description: During Ragnarök, Sköll will finally catch and devour Sol, plunging the world into darkness. This event marks the beginning of the end.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A primary source for Norse mythology, including descriptions of Sol and her role.
sources.text: A primary source for Norse mythology, including descriptions of Sol and her role.

sources.author: Various
sources.description: A collection of Old Norse poems that contain mythological stories, including references to Sol.
